title: MGMT MEMO Volume 7, Number 5 — Digital's State of the Company Meeting
author: Digital Equipment Corporation (Richard Seltzer, editor)
abstract: mgmtmemo7_5.html, Summaries of nine executive speeches delivered at Digital Equipment Corporation's May 5, 1988 State of the Company Meeting in Merrimack, NH, attended by over 600 senior managers. The theme was 'One Company, One Strategy, One Message — Leading the Way to Enterprise-Wide Computing,' with major sections on desktop/workstation strategy, transaction processing, DECwindows, database systems, and VAXcluster high availability. This document captures DEC at its revenue peak (~$11B, 120K+ employees) articulating a bold enterprise strategy that proved partially prescient and partially fatal.
importance_rationale: Captures DEC's entire executive leadership team — president Ken Olsen, VP Finance, VP Product Strategy, and six senior managers — articulating corporate strategy at the company's absolute revenue and market-cap zenith in mid-1988. Uniquely rich primary source for understanding the minicomputer era's culmination.
relevance_rationale: Foundational document for understanding the minicomputer-to-enterprise computing transition, DEC's strategic miscalculations versus IBM and the PC wave, and the organizational dynamics that led to DEC's eventual collapse. Essential reading for technology historians, strategy scholars, and anyone studying platform competition.
prescience_rationale: Mixed: DECtp and networking predictions proved correct; desktop/PC dismissal and DECwindows-as-standard proved fatally wrong.
